methods.description: Process for measuring water stable isotope ratios (δ18O and δD) using a Picarro L2120-i analyzer at the Climate and Environmental Research Laboratory (CERL) and the Laboratory of Isotope Hydrology of the IAEA.

steps.description: Water stable isotope ratios (δ18O and δD) were measured at the Climate and Environmental Research Laboratory (CERL) at the Arctic and Antarctic Research Institute (St Petersburg, Russia), using a Picarro L2120-i analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water stable isotope ratios using Picarro L2120-i analyzer

steps.description: Sequences of measurements included the injection of 5 samples, followed by the injection of an internal laboratory standard with an isotopic value close to that of the samples.
steps.searchable_summary: inject samples and standards for isotope measurement

steps.description: Repeated the measurements of about 10% of all the samples in order to calculate the analytical precision: 0.06‰ for δ18O and 0.30‰ for δD.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate analytical precision by repeating measurements

steps.description: 600 samples from the depth interval from 23 to 35 m were measured in the Laboratory of Isotope Hydrology of the IAEA (Vienna, Austria).
steps.searchable_summary: measure isotope ratios at IAEA for depth interval 23-35 m

steps.description: The two records are highly correlated (r=0.99, p < 0.05) for both isotopes with a systematic offset of 0.2 ‰ for δ18O and 1 ‰ for δD.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation between CERL and IAEA isotope records

methods.description: Procedures for sampling ice cores using classic cutting-melting and continuous flow analysis (CFA) systems, with varying resolutions to ensure adequate sample collection.

steps.description: Sampling was performed using classic cutting-melting procedures for the upper and lower parts of the deep core and for the shallow firn cores drilled in 2012 and 2013.
For other depth intervals, melted samples were extracted from the continuous flow analysis system of LGGE, automatically sub-sampled, frozen, and stored in vials.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and store samples using CFA system

steps.description: The sampling resolution was 15 cm for the upper 16 m of the deep core, increased to 5 cm from 16 to 70 m depth and in the bottom part of the core, and to 4 cm from 70 to 106 m.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust sampling resolution for different core depths

methods.method_name: Comparison of Ice Core Records with Regional Meteorological Data
methods.description: Method for comparing ice core data with regional meteorological data and large-scale modes of variability to analyze correlations and reconstruct past precipitation rates.

steps.description: Compared the ice core data with the regional meteorological data and the large-scale modes of variability.
steps.searchable_summary: compare ice core data with meteorological data

steps.description: Established a linear regression model for the period 1966-2013 to reconstruct past precipitation rates for the Klukhorsky Pereval station.
steps.searchable_summary: establish linear regression model for precipitation reconstruction

steps.description: Calculation of the seasonal cycle of precipitation isotopic composition using the LMDZiso model.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate seasonal cycle of precipitation isotopic composition
